title: rhododendron aureum subsp. aureum
intro: Rhododendron aureum subsp. aureum, also known as the Golden Azalea, is a subspecies of flowering plant that is native to the southeastern United States. The Golden Azalea has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and salves, to provide health benefits like reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Rhododendron aureum subsp. aureum are also used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
